entries.d: A long time without rain, when the land becomes very dry
entries.s: The long _____ lasted for six months and all the rivers, lakes, and wells in the area dried up completely.

entries.d: When the ground shakes suddenly and strongly

entries.d: A very strong storm with fast winds and heavy rain
entries.s: The powerful tropical _____ brought extremely strong spiral winds of 200 km/h and destroyed many wooden houses on the Caribbean island.

entries.d: When a volcano throws out hot rock and lava
entries.s: The huge _____ from Mount Vesuvius covered the whole Roman village of Pompeii in a thick layer of hot grey ash from the sky.

entries.d: When wet earth moves quickly down a hill or mountain
entries.s: After three days of heavy rain on the hill, a big brown _____ of wet earth and rocks blocked the main road to the village.

entries.d: A large amount of snow falling quickly down a mountain
entries.s: The three skiers on the mountain had to be rescued by helicopter after a huge _____ of snow came crashing down the slope.

entries.d: A large blaze that burns trees in the woods
entries.s: The terrible _____ destroyed thousands of tall pine trees in the woods in just one single day during the hot dry summer.

entries.d: A very large ocean wave caused by an earthquake under the sea
entries.s: The enormous _____ wave from the ocean flooded the entire coast and destroyed many beach homes and shops.

entries.d: When water covers land that is usually dry
entries.s: The heavy river _____ after three days of rain covered the whole street with half a metre of brown water and came into our house through the door.

entries.d: A practice exercise so everyone knows what to do in an emergency
entries.s: We have a practice _____ at school every few months to teach us how to safely evacuate the building in case of a real emergency.

entries.d: The way out of a building in an emergency
entries.s: Always look carefully for the nearest _____ and exit signs when you enter a new unfamiliar building like a cinema or hotel.

entries.d: A small device on the ceiling that makes a loud noise when something is burning
entries.s: Every bedroom in a house should have a _____ attached to the ceiling that beeps loudly when it senses smoke from a fire.

entries.d: A location where everyone gathers after an emergency
entries.s: Our designated _____ after every school fire drill is the far corner of the car park behind the gym.

entries.d: To make sure all entrances are closed and safe
entries.s: Before you leave your house for holiday, always _____ carefully to make sure they are all properly locked.

entries.d: To move on your hands and knees close to the floor
entries.s: If there is thick smoke filling a room during a fire, _____ on your hands and knees to stay below the smoke where the air is cleaner.

entries.d: A safety rule: stay still, go to the ground, and turn your body if you catch fire
entries.s: If your clothes catch fire, remember to immediately _____ on the ground to put out the flames without running.

entries.d: The careful study of a subject to find new facts
entries.s: Scientists around the world are doing important _____ with special equipment to try and understand earthquakes better and predict them.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with RESEARCH?

entries.d: To be stuck somewhere and unable to get out
entries.s: The poor black cat was _____ high up on the top branch of the tall tree and couldn't get down by itself without help.

entries.d: The force of pushing against something
entries.s: The enormous _____ of the rushing river water finally broke through the old concrete dam wall after days of heavy rain.

entries.d: The outside or top part of something
entries.s: The _____ of the calm quiet lake was completely still and smooth like glass — there were no waves or ripples at all.

entries.d: Below the surface of the sea
entries.s: There are many active _____ volcanoes deep under the Pacific Ocean waves that we can't see from the boats above the waves.

entries.d: The line that separates two countries
entries.s: We slowly crossed the _____ line from Austria into Germany by car yesterday, showing our passports to the checkpoint guard.

entries.d: Harm done to something so it is broken or doesn't work well
entries.s: The huge windy storm last night caused a lot of serious _____ to the roof of our school — several tiles fell off.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with DAMAGE?

entries.d: To move people away from a dangerous place
entries.s: The police officers with loudspeakers _____ all the people from the dangerous burning building quickly and safely within ten minutes.

entries.d: To find out the size, weight or amount of something
entries.s: We _____ the whole classroom carefully with a long metal measuring tape to see exactly how big it is in square metres.

entries.d: A large area of land
entries.s: This beautiful mountainous _____ of Austria called Tyrol is famous for its stunning lakes, snowy Alps, and ski resorts.

entries.d: Using force to hurt people or things
entries.s: The terrible thunderstorm became much more _____ as the night went on, with very strong winds and lightning striking the ground.

entries.d: To not go near something dangerous
entries.s: _____ the edge of the steep cliff at the seaside — it's very dangerous and you could easily slip and fall all the way down.

entries.d: To drop to the ground from a higher position
entries.s: The old 100-year-old oak tree in our garden _____ with a loud crash during the strong storm last night.

entries.d: To suddenly understand or notice something
entries.s: I suddenly _____ at the dinner table that it was already 9 pm — I had completely lost track of time playing video games all afternoon.

entries.d: Staying alive in a dangerous situation
entries.s: The exciting book tells the dramatic _____ story of a 12-year-old girl who was lost alone in the Alps mountains for three days.

entries.d: Below or under something
entries.s: The shy black cat was hiding _____ the big wooden bed in the bedroom, just above the floor where nobody could see her.

entries.d: The grey powder left after something has burned
entries.s: After the huge campfire burned down at midnight, there was only grey _____ and some black charcoal left on the ground in the morning.

entries.d: A person who is left alone on an island after their ship sinks
entries.s: The lonely _____ lived completely alone on the deserted tropical island for two long years after his ship sank in the storm.

entries.d: To bring something to a person or place
entries.s: The friendly postman _____ letters, postcards, and small parcels to every single house on our street every morning at 9 am.

entries.d: A business that brings things to people's houses
entries.s: The express _____ brought my new pair of running shoes to my front door in just one day after I ordered them online.

entries.d: The bright, hot part of a fire
entries.s: We all watched the orange and yellow _____ of the outdoor campfire dance and flicker in the dark night.

entries.d: To become comfortable with something new or different
entries.s: I quickly _____ wearing my new glasses after a few days — at first they felt strange and heavy, but now they feel normal.

entries.d: The town or city where you grew up
entries.s: She moved away to another country ten years ago, but she still visits her old _____ where she grew up every single summer.

entries.d: A feeling of great happiness
entries.s: The excited children all jumped up and down with pure _____ when they heard exciting news about the surprise trip to Disneyland.

entries.d: A package that is sent or delivered
entries.s: There's a brown cardboard _____ with your name on the label at the front door — the postman must have delivered it this morning.

entries.d: A flat structure made of wood for floating on water
entries.s: They built a simple floating _____ out of old pieces of wood and rope tied together to cross the wide river.

entries.d: A safe place to stay, especially from bad weather
entries.s: They quickly found a safe _____ under a big rock overhang when it suddenly started to pour with heavy rain during their hike.

entries.d: To become something completely different
entries.s: The cold winter rain _____ into soft white snow as the temperature dropped below zero degrees at night.

entries.d: Something amazing that you can't easily explain
entries.s: It was an absolute _____ that nobody was seriously hurt in the huge car accident on the motorway — everyone walked away safely.

entries.d: A remote, uninhabited piece of land surrounded by water
entries.s: If I were stuck on a _____ with nothing around me, I would bring a fishing rod to catch fish and a match to make a fire.

entries.d: A feeling of enjoyment and happiness
entries.s: It gives me great _____ and makes me feel warm inside to help other kind people who need help with their homework.

entries.d: If something happens, if there is a...
entries.s: _____ emergency fire, please use the stairs, not the lift which might get stuck between floors.

entries.d: A small object that makes a flame
entries.s: My dad uses a small plastic _____ to start the charcoal barbecue fire in the back garden on sunny weekend afternoons.

entries.d: To fall down suddenly
entries.s: The old stone wall in the garden finally _____ after years of rain, wind, and weathering damaged its structure and weakened the mortar.
